title: Phenomenon 400

{{Underline|fff}} {{Underline|fff}}
✵
'''Bleeding Stars'''

{{Underline|fff}} {{Underline|fff}} [[File:BlackHolelowres2.png|750px|link=|center]]
I
OVERVIEW
''Bleeding Stars'', among other namesThese names being: To Make God Bleed, Angel's Eyes, Boiling Stars, Dawn of the Void, Call of the Void and the Rapture's Evening., is the colloquial term given to '''Phenomenon 400''', an exceptionally rare phenomenon of [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]] in which a celestial body within a [[Level List|level]] is temporarily replaced with a black hole. While this is typically the [[The Frontrooms|sun-or-moon]]-equivalent of the level, it can be any notable object within the level's sky, as shown by the occurrence of this phenomenon on [[Level 387|Sky Ferris Wheels]]. The phenomenon consists of the spatial object, titled the "''Wounded''", slowly melting away as it rises and sets, until approximately one week later, where instead of the ''Wounded'' rising, it is instead a black hole, sometimes called the "''Clot''". The ''Clot'' has little correlation to the ''Wounded'', typically being closer to the level's floor than the ''Wounded'' was and having wildly varying mass. While the ''Clot'' is in the sky, the level will experience significant gravitational changes, often negating the effects of [[Phenomenon 3|Antigravity]], though the severity of these changes depends on the distance and size of the ''Clot''. Once the ''Clot'' fully sets, the celestial object will reappear in the same state as before the occurrence of the phenomenon, though the level itself will still maintain structural damage from the gravitational changes. Beyond the fact the phenomenon only occurs in levels containing a sky (including a sun/moon-proxy and/or [[Aterno Momentum|constellations]]), current documentations of the phenomena show little consistency in the manifestation of '''Phenomena 400''', with the lowest time period between reports being 10 months and the highest at 61 years. In addition, the cause of the phenomena is yet to be confirmed with some [[M.E.G.]] researchers chalking it up to [[Phenomenon 11|instability]] in the 'skies' of levels. {{Phenomenon Class Alt|width=80%|overall=3|frequency=1|danger=3|understand=4|scale=3|icon=|freqtext=There have only been 6 properly documented instances of ''Bleeding Stars'' since 1889|dangtext=The danger posed by this phenomenon varies significantly depending on the severity of the ''Rupture'' (See {II-II}). Phase 1 & 3 are negligible in deadliness|undertext=Though the phenomenon's effects have been thoroughly documented, the underlying cause is unknown|scaletext=The phenomenon is self-contained within the affected level. However, this level undergoes drastic changes that can take weeks to months to be fully reversed}}


II
PHASES
As implied earlier, the phenomena has several distinct phases. This article will only cover 3 phases, even though [[The Kauer Research Organization|some]] argue that ''Haemorrhage'' should be split up into 2 smaller phases. These phases- in order- are:
:''Haemorrhage'' :''Rupture'' :''Scars''
Each phase marks a drastic shift in the phenomenon's behaviour. Haemorrhage signifies the beginning, where the first noticeable effects happen to the ''Wounded'' and the sky. Phase 2 marks the climax of the phenomenon; when ''Bleeding Stars'' is at its deadliest. ''Scars'' is the aftermath of the onset, focusing on the lasting effects to the level.
II - I
HAEMORRHAGE
The first phase of ''Bleeding Stars'' is "''Haemorrhage''". This phase does not have any significant effects on the level itself, primarily being visual changes to the sky of the affected level.
[[File:BleedingStars1.png|link=|500px]]{{Underline|fff}}An image of a ''Wounded'' moon, taken in the [[Level 894|Crystal Fields]].
On the first day the celestial body becomes ''Wounded'', a singular, large explosive sound will echo across the entire level. The blast clocks in at ~115dB, producing notable strain on the listener's eardrums (though never shown to be permanently damaging). Curiously, the soundwave does not diminish with distance; it maintains full intensity and volume even though it is covering near-infinite or even [[Phenomenon 14|non-euclidean]] spans of land. Based off the research conducted on the ''Wounded'' in [[Level 387]], the sound is likely created by a sudden release of internal pressure within the spatial body. From this point onwards, every time the ''Wounded'' becomes visible, it will appear to be bleeding. That is to say, as the moon, star or planet continues its movement or orbital path, it leaves behind an incandescent trail of superheated plasma It is currently unknown if the jettisoned matter is in fact plasma or something else such as deliquesced [[Object 15|cinder crystal]], though plasma is the most common assumption.. The density and volume of the material released increases as ''Haemorrhage'' progresses, causing the surrounding sky to become increasingly more bright. This, paired with the ''Wounded'' 's increased luminosity itself, can sometimes be blinding to a wanderer even if not directly stared at. The ''Wounded'' 's "blood" rarely moves, only fading slowly or breaking away into smaller globules. However, sometimes, the plasma will not dissipate, but rather stay stranded in mid-air, eventually forming a crack-like structure in the sky. This structure appears to be a side-effect of the phenomenon rather than a part of it, due to the fact that the crack remains visible for several months or years after the 'Rupture' has concluded.
[[File:BleedingStars2.webp|link=]]{{Underline|fff}}An image of a ''Wounded'' Sun, captured by the [[Entity 1|Cameraman]] in an unknown level.
During ''Haemorrhage'', there are also some technological changes. For one, radios, walkie-talkies and sometimes more advanced electronics like [[Phenomenon 2|terminals]] are slightly muffled or glitchy from static interference. Another change is the malfunctioning of anything requiring magnetic fields. Compasses, magnetometers and certain [[Major Navigation System#LPS (Liminal Positioning System) |LPS]] devices all break within a level suffering from '''Phenomenon 400'''. This is believed to be in due part because of the ejected plasma containing large amounts of iron that could create powerful but random magnetic fields. However, both of these changes will only minorly affect wanderers. ''Haemorrhage'' takes place for approximately a week before the ''Rupture'' commences, though the exact timeframe varies from level-to-level since most levels with a dual-celestial cycle have differing day/night durations to each other and [[The Frontrooms|reality]]. Furthermore, as Phase 2 encroaches, the amount of time the ''Wounded'' remains in the sky increases substantially. As a result, on more dangerous levels, this phase could pose a far deadlier threat as it may lead to [[Entity List|entities]] such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]] and many others hunting for prey for longer. If a wanderer finds themselves in a level where ''Haemorrhage'' is ensuing, the best course of action is to leave as quick as possible. The possible danger posed by Phase 2 will almost certainly outweigh any desire to explore, document or reside within the level at that given moment. If possible, it is also recommended to alert [[M.E.G.|us]] or any other [[Groups|group]] of the fact that the phenomenon is occurring so we may protect other wanderers and possibly conduct research on the phenomenon too.
⠀
II - II
RUPTURE
The second, and arguably most notorious, phase of Phenomenon 400 is the "Rupture", sometimes also referred to as "God's Wounds" or "Occlusion" . During this phase, the Wounded is replaced into a black hole, or ''Clot'', causing significant gravitational fluctuations across the entirety of the level.
[[File:A_view_of_Gargantua_from_inside_the_planet..png|link=|1000px]]{{Underline|fff}}Another [[Entity 1|Cameraman]]-sourced image, this one depicted a rising ''Clot''.
[[File:BleedingStars3.jpg|link=|500px]]{{Underline|fff}}An image of the largest recorded ''Clot'', photographed in 1989 where [[Level 150|Flowers Bloom]].
[[File:BleedingStars4.webp|link=|250px]]{{Underline|fff}}Another image of a ''Clot'' captured in the [[Level 46|Arabian Desert]]. Note the presence of debris in mid-air.
As mentioned in {II - I}, this phase typically commences a week after the phenomenon as a whole begins. By this point, the trail left by the ''Wounded'' will be substantially long- almost trailing the entire sky- and there will likely be a multitude of cracks present in the sky. However, after the ''Wounded'' sinks beyond the "horizon", its next return (as the ''Clot'') will mark the onset of ''Occlusion''. As the ''Clot'' rises, immediate changes in the level can be seen/detected. The technological glitches seen during ''Haemorrhage'' are significantly amplified: radios and the like only relay loud static noise, while [[Phenomenon 2|terminals]] and the installed [[BackOS]] are far more glitchy, often malfunctioning to the point of inoperability. In addition, magnetic-field reliant devices entirely malfunction within the affected level, though other devices such as HDDs and keycards have also been known to break during the phenomenon. The ''Clot'' itself appears to be a regular stellar [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_hole black hole], though all instances of the ''Clot'' also have an accretion disk. This disk, especially at the magnitude that most ''Clots'' possess, should not be possible, as only a handful of asteroids and other objects have ever been documented in [[A Timeline of the Backrooms|Backrooms history]]In the case of the [[Phenomenon 8|planetary-based]] ''Clot'' on [[Level 387]], it was clear that a large part of it's accretion disk was created by the tidal disruption and subsequent accretion of the previous moons of the object. However, the ''Clot'' still had a prior accretion disk before the absorption of the satellites. and certainly far fewer would be available for accretion by the ''Clot''. It is unknown if the ''Clot'' has similar effects to that of a [[The Frontrooms|real]] black hole, such as spaghettification, though evidence such as the distortion of light about the ''Clot'' and the gravitational effects on the level and other objects strongly suggests that (at least) most of its physical effects are functionally identical. The ''Clot'' will follow the typical orbital trajectory of it's ''Wounded'' original counterpart, the only difference being the substantial decrease in speed (though this was stated in {II - I}). However, as the ''Clot'' moves, small ejecta will be released from the accretion disk of the black hole. These ejecta-labelled as coagulates- are small incandescent orbs or steams of plasma that have been released into the [[The Void|surrounding area]] by the ''Clot''. Rarely do this ejecta reach the surface, due to the fact that they often dissipate when entering the atmosphere of the level. As coagulates fall, they rapidly expel an enormous amount of heat, which can cause anything, such as people and edifices, in a nearby vicinity to be incinerated if the ejecta does manage to reach/get near to the surface of the level. However, the most well-known impact of the ''Clot'' as it moves through the sky are it's gravitational changes. During the ''Rupture'', gravity within the affected level will fluctuate, getting either far weaker (=> Objects floating Objects could also be pulled up by the gravity of the ''Clot'' too, though this is only at high severity.), or slightly stronger (=>Objects being crushed). In both cases, these changes will not effect present wanderers or [[Entity List|entities]] aswell as integral parts of the level's composition, such as the floorResearchers attribute this selective anchoring to how levels are composed, as a lack of biological matter as well large parts of spatial matter is theorised to cause the decay of a level.. However, objects- especially unanchored objects, though even entire edifices- will be effected. Though objects will be almost immediately lifted or crushed, buildings about to be pulled or compressed are indicated through rapid vibrations and formations of cracks in the walls and floor. Though wanderers are unaffected by the spacetime warping, they can be indirectly effected by being lifted by objects they are on, or being crushed by objects falling from above/the roof above collapsing. In addition, wanderers may experience severe vertigo or nausea. Due to the gravity of the ''Clot''', the atmosphere of a level is significantly changed: winds have been known to reach substantially high speeds; storms, particularly tornadoes, have been to form across the entire level; slight temperature and pressure shifts were noted on [[Level 387|Sky Ferris Wheels]], and thus it is believed that this also occurs on all other instances of '''Phenomenon 400'''. Cumulatively, these changes alone can cause significant casualties, as debris and wanderers can be thrown around by these changes in winds. That said, these changes all depend on the severity of the ''Clot''. A ''Clot'''s severity level depends on it's approximate distance away from the level, as well as its approximate mass. The more massive a ''Clot'', the more severe gravitational distortions and atmospherically changes will occur. Conversely, the closer a ''Clot'' is to the level, the more frequent such changes are. Though not as important, the surrounding objects in the sky could also add to the danger by allowing for the formation of coagulates. Severity levels also vary slightly based of the duration within the ''Rupture''; while the ''Clot'' is directly overhead, spacetime effects are at their peak, though the closer the ''Clot'' is to the level's horizon, the weaker these effects are.
⠀
II - III
SCARS
[[File:BleedingStars5.webp|link=|400px]]{{Underline|fff}}An image of a crack in the sky anomalously provided to the [[M.E.G.]] by a wanderer, likely taken in the [[Level 894|Crystal Fields]].
''Occlusion'' fully comes to an end once the ''Clot'' sets and the opposing celestial body rises. From this point on, the effected ''Wounded'' will return to its original state, prior to the occurrence of ''Bleeding Stars''. However, the phenomenon will still have several long-lasting effects on the level. While not technically part of the actual phenomenon, these remaining impacts of ''Bleeding Stars'' on affected levels are still noteworthy, and thus has been dubbed as "''Scars''".
[[File:BleedingStars6.jpg|link=|400px]]{{Underline|fff}}The view of [[Level 270]] the following day of the ''Rupture''.
The first impact on the level is structural damage. Due to gravitational changes during the ''Rupture'', buildings, objects, and plant-life will have been scattered and thus destroyed due to being lifted or have collapsed in on themselves from being pushed downwards. Either way, '''Phenomenon 400''' has been known to leave levels in significant states of disrepair in the immediate time following the effects of the ''Clot''. In addition, ejecta released by the ''Clot'' may have led to ongoing fires and combustion, though these are typically short-lived. Long-term, these changes are slowly reverted through a process of ''Level regeneration'', akin to that of a level [[Phenomenon 106|destabilising]]. The time-period it takes for a level to regenerate depends on the impact the phenomenon had on the level, though likely also randomly varies on a per-level basis. From ''Haemorrhage'', left-over cracks in the sky left by the ''Wounded'' have been recorded to last for years, if not decades, in the sky. Analysis of these cracks reveal that they are simply strands of plasma that have been anonymously "glued" in space and time. As such, the only way that these cracks will disappear is through the significant slowed dissipation of energy leading to recombination of the material into gas. Fortunately, these cracks show pose no additional threat within a level, due to the fact that no coagulates have ever been reported to have been released by the crack. A final effect is the presence of gravitationally [[Phenomenon 11|unstable]] regions in a level, still negating the re-established [[Phenomenon 3|gravity]] within the level. Only 2 occurrences of this have been found, with only 1 of these still remaining to this day.These are [[Level 488|Mankind's Worst Creation]] and the [[Level 894|Crystal Fields]], with [[Level 488]]'s residuum being the one that is still active till this document's writing (26 / 07 / 2021) Within these zones, gravity is far more unstable, leading to placed objects in this zone to be pulled down more rapidly than usual, fall more slowly than usual, or experience a 'negative' g-force, causing it to rise up. In addtion, this property- unlike during the ''Rupture''- can affect wanderers too, but only if they are not on the level's floor while in the region (e.g.: In mid-air after a jump).



III
DOCUMENTATIONS
Below is a list of documentations of ''Bleeding Stars'' within the [[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]], in descending order. Proper documentation does not exist for some of the older entries, so some inference has been made on details. *Note that all dates used are based off the [[Timeline of the Backrooms|Frontrooms Date Theory]]
{| class="wikitable" style="width:75%%; text-align:center; border-collapse:collapse;" |- | style="padding:0; vertical-align:top; width:50%" |
Level 387
Date: 12 / 05 / 2019
Wounded: Distant Planet, titled "Erebi" after the fact.
Severity: 0.2 / 10 - Low mass as well as a High distance

{{Modal|number=1|linkcolor=orange|linkstyle=display:inline-block; background:black; padding:8px 14px; margin:6px 0 0 0; border:2px solid white; border-radius:0 10px 10px 0|open=> Open P400_L387.zip|close=> Close File|width=75rem|style=border:2px solid orange|content=The most recent occurrence of Phenomenon 400 was on [[Level 387]], Sky Ferris Wheels. As mentioned earlier, this was the first time the phenomenon was indisputably affecting a planet rather than a sun‑or‑moon proxy. Prior to its collapse, ErebiTitled as such after 'Erebus', meaning darkness, reflecting the planet's conversion into a ''Clot.'' was a terrestrial super‑Earth with a brown-basalt‑coloured surface and an unusually strong gravitational field. This was most apparent in its two minor moons, which orbited in visibly unstable, frantic paths. These observations- along with the widespread tectonic fracturing visible across the planet’s surface- were considered quite peculiar by [[Architect Industries]], who had been analysing Erebi as a potential candidate for colonisation. In hindsight, these instabilities were likely symptomatic of deeper, unidentified forces within the planet's interior, precursors to the "bleeding" and shockwaves seen in ''Haemorrhage''.
Due to Erebi's immense distance from [[Level 387]] and the relatively small size of the resulting Clot, the only effects on the level were minor technological glitches and atmospheric changes. In [[Phenomenon 8|space]], however, the planet’s two moons were completely dismantled and accreted into the Clot's disk- a change that proved permanent, as the moons did not return when Erebi re‑formed. This instance of Angel’s Eyes nevertheless allowed for far more detailed analysis of the phenomenon due to improved [[Object List|observational technologies]]. Notably, the transformation of a Wounded into a Clot, and its subsequent restoration, was confirmed to occur through a rapid process of gravitational implosion followed by a rebound of matter. Another confirmed detail was that the "blood" released by a Wounded, as well as its characteristic glow, originates from the expulsion of the body’s internal fluid.
[[File:Erebi better.png|217px|link=]]
'''An image of Erebi prior to Phenomenon
400, provided by [[Apollo-∞]]'''
[[File:Black hole.png|250px|link=]]
'''An image of Erebi in ''Clot''-form'''
}}
| style="padding:0; vertical-align:top; width:50%" |
Level 894
Date: 01 / 04 / 2001
Wounded: Moon
Severity: 6.7 / 10 - Fairly massive and Fairly Close

{{Modal|number=2|linkcolor=orange|linkstyle=display:inline-block; background:black; padding:8px 14px; margin:6px 0 0 0; border:2px solid white; border-radius:0 10px 10px 0|open=> Open P400_L894.zip|close=> Close File|width=75rem|style=border:2px solid orange;|content=This occurrence of '''Phenomenon 400''' was the most regular, following all patterns understood from previous documentations. The phenomenon took place in the [[Level 894|Crystal Fields]] during it's winter, which lead to scenic skies during ''Haemorrhage'' as it was the moon that was ''Wounded''. The length of night increase from it's ~30 hours to about ~60 hours, making it have one of the longest duration for every single phase of ''Bleeding Stars''. Interestingly, the trail released by the ''Wounded'' had an interesting effect on the TS2 crystals of the level, with all of the crystal's photoluminescence spiking at the ''Wounded'' 's presence. Whether this was due to reflections or an unknown property is yet to be found.
During the ''Rupture'', wind, especially in the wasteland, increased to hurricane-like levels, with many windmills being lifted into the air, some being thrown back on the level causing calamity-like incidents since (despite their durability) the turbines were often completely dismantled while in the sky. Due to the barren nature of [[Level 894]], the phenomenon barely posed a threat, with the level's [[Entities]] still being the most important concern for most. After the ''Rupture'' had concluded, several cracks remained in the sky. There was also an area of gravitational distortion, making it the 2nd time this region was spotted. Besides that, the level completely healed from ''Bleeding Stars'' in about a year.
[[File:Windmill broken.jpg|link=|400px]]
'''A broken windmill found months after the
phenomenon concluded, likely from the wind.'''
}}
|- | style="padding:0; vertical-align:top; width:50%" |
Level 150
Date: 28 / 11 / 1989
Wounded: Moon
Severity: 5 / 10 - Near-Ultramassive however Very far from level

{{Modal|number=3|linkcolor=orange|linkstyle=display:inline-block; background:black; padding:8px 14px; margin:6px 0 0 0; border:2px solid white; border-radius:0 10px 10px 0|open=> Open P400_L150.zip|min-height=15rem|close=> Close File|width=75rem|style=border:2px solid orange;|content=Likely the most known occurrence of ''Bleeding Stars'' was the conversion of the moon of [[Level 150]] into a Black Hole. This Black Hole was also the largest ever recorded from the phenomenon, at approximately 1 light year in diameter, which would be just shy of 17 million M☉. It is believed that if the black hole was the same distance away as the ''Wounded'' and Moon of the level originally was, the sheer tidal force of the level would have overcome the gravitational binding force of the level's floor, leading to the utter destruction of the level, leaving it as just a [[The Void|void]]. Luckily, the Black hole was astronomically far, which meant that the gravitational forces were almost completely neutered, with only the occasional tree or lamppost levitating or being crushed, rendering the entire phenomenon a spectacle carved in the sky.
Because the phenomenon happened on such an integral and populous level of [[Clusters|Cluster I]], it opened many wanderer's eyes to the phenomenon, making it the revered phenomenon it is today. Some groups even began to worship the Black Hole as a deity, believing it is humanity's destiny to become one with it. This happening of the phenomenon is also seen as the genesis of proper Phenomology, a branch of Levology dedicated to studying and documenting [[Phenomenon List|phenomena]] in particular; before this point, only a handful of scientists took note of any phenomena beyond [[Phenomenon 15|loneliness]], [[No-Clipping|no-clipping]], [[Phenomenon 14|peculiar geometry]] and [[Phenomenon 11|glitches]]. }}
| style="padding:0; vertical-align:top; width:50%" |
Level 488
Date: 17 / 08 / 1928
Wounded: Sun
Severity: 7.4 / 10 - Substantially large though fairly distanced

{{Modal|number=4|linkcolor=orange|linkstyle=display:inline-block; background:black; padding:8px 14px; margin:6px 0 0 0; border:2px solid white; border-radius:0 10px 10px 0|open=> Open P400_L488.zip|min-height=30rem|close=> Close File|width=75rem|style=border:2px solid orange|content='''Phenomenon 400''' happening on [[Level 488|our mistake]] is confirmed, based off of 2 different sources recording it. However, actual details of the event are unknown, due to how long ago this was. Of these 2 sources, one was a short letter sent to [[M.E.G.donalds (Joke)|us]] by an anonymous individual enquiring as to the "sudden gravitational changes" and "sudden appearance of a Black Sun" in the sky. Unfortunately, this letter was believed to be a falsity by us back then, as it appeared to be an attempt at fame or to siphon the group's resources. The discovery of a diary belonging to "Robert K. Woodhouse" (a WW1 veteran) approximately 6 years later, however, proved this letter correct. The diary described Woodhouse's life in the BackrooMs, with the last few pages documenting his time in [[Level 488]], where it is believed he had died. Unlike the typical description of the level to what is now-and-was-then known, it is obvious that Woodhouse entered the level just before and during ''Bleeding Stars''.
The diary describes all major events of ''Bleeding Stars'', including: "a singular, savage tremor", describing the preliminary shockwave of ''haemorrhage''; the "fissures" in the sky either referencing the bleeding Sun or the cracks left by it; it also mentions "droplets of the cosmos raining down", maybe in reference to the released plasma coagulates, though it is vague; and lastly, the "charcoal maw" and it's "pull on the level" obviously depicting the ''Clot'' and it's effect on the level. The final line of the diary states "It's happening again: the same screams just before the shells fell on Ypres, the ones that made the Earth itself carnivorous. Only know the mud and body-filled buildings are lifting with me. I know this is the end. God help me, I am too tired to run. God help us all ", indicating Woodhouse succumbed to his death shortly after, with the upward smearing of the ink and erratic handwriting supporting this interpretation. It is of note that Woodhouse mentions the 'Clot' had "two fiery plumes, like torches held to the heavens, jutting from its poles", a description of (what many believe to be) a quasar. This could suggest that the ''Clot''s formed by the phenomenon can have more than just an accretion disk, though this is the only time that anything similar to what Woodhouse described has been noted. }}
|- | style="padding:0; vertical-align:top; width:50%" |
Level 270
Date: 29 / 09 / 1927
Wounded: Sun
Severity: 9.3 / 10 - Very large and very close

{{Modal|number=5|linkcolor=orange|linkstyle=display:inline-block; background:black; padding:8px 14px; margin:6px 0 0 0; border:2px solid white; border-radius:0 10px 10px 0|open=> Open P400_L270.zip|min-height=30rem|close=> Close File|width=75rem|style=border:2px solid orange;|content=Prior to '''Level 150''', the most infamous time that '''Phenomenon 400''' was documented was on [[Level 270]], though at the time, it was either believed to be a one-time occurrence, or a [[Phenomenon List|level-specific phenomenon]], as the [[Level 46]] occurrence was largely forgotten and earlier documentation (even now, to a degree) was not found. This is believed to be the deadliest instance of ''Bleeding Stars'', in due part because of the immense magnitude of the ''Clot'' and it's proximity. During this event, the entire level was left destroyed, with many structures being reported to have been pulled into (what is now known as) the event horizon. Death tolls are estimated to be in the thousands if not 10s of thousands, which would have been a significant population this far back in time.
While there is little clear-cut information on the event, it is known that the event made the night of the level significantly shorter to approximately 40 hours, allowing for the ''Wounded'' to be seen more oftenTechnically speaking, this was the first time the 'Sun' of the level was seen, though these are excluded since it was caused by this phenomenon, not from the level itself. Besides this external event, daytime was only seen once.. Furthermore, the entire city faced electrical disruption throughout the event, such as on the night before the ''Rupture'', in which the level underwent a 3 hour black-out. During Phase 2 of the phenomenon, several buildings were pulled down into the ground, which due to the close proximity of all the edifices, led to a domino effect that led to large areas of the city plummeting and mass death. This is made worse by the fog being pulled up and down by the gravitational effects in such erratic movements that tornadoes and hurricanes began to form, and some debris being launched across streets. Buildings that were pulled upwards instead would either be engulfed by the ''Clot'' or fall back down in cataclysmic waves of fire and rock, turning the level's atmosphere into a furnace of sorts and causing even more immense destruction. The sole survivor put it as "the level being torn apart, screaming as it met it's end." After the chaos settled, the level was left in complete ruins, still environmentally deadly to be in, as rubble could, at any second, suddenly flow outward like an avalanche while the floor of the level itself could collapse and lead to [[The Void]]. It is estimated to have taken 15-and-a-bit years for the level to fully heal, ±2 years.
Though [[Phenomenon 15|loneliness]] permeates through the Foggy Skyscrapers, the level at the time was believed to have housed 1000s of wanderers. Since only 1 survivor was found, it is assumed that all others die as no-one could help save them. It is possible that the phenomenon was slightly different based off which copy of [[Level 270]] they were in, however this is unknown. From the oral source we have, this instance of ''Boiling Stars'' was by-far the deadliest of all manifestations. }}
| style="padding:0; vertical-align:top; width:50%" |
Level 46
Date: 11 / 12 / 1889
Wounded: Unknown
Severity: 7.1 / 10 - Decently small but significantly close

{{Modal|number=6|linkcolor=orange|linkstyle=display:inline-block; background:black; padding:8px 14px; margin:6px 0 0 0; border:2px solid white; border-radius:0 10px 10px 0|open=> Open P400_L046.zip|min-height=25rem|close=> Close File|width=75rem|style=border:2px solid orange;|content=The oldest properly confirmed instance of '''Phenomenon 400''' was on the [[Level 46|Arabian Desert]]. It is unknown what the wounded was, though it is known that it was ridiculously close to the level, so much so that the sand of the level itself would be pulled up and form dust-storms. However, since [[Level 46]] is quite barren, this was just about the only impact the phenomenon had.
Because this event predates organised exploration by decades, documentation is extremely limited. The M.E.G. archives contain only a handful of fragmented sources: *Oral tales from early wanderer communities, many of which have likely been embellished over generations, and some of which slightly contradict each other. *A single blurred photograph of the ''Clot'', believed (though not confirmed) to have been taken by [[Entity 1|the Cameraman]] (See {II-II} for image). *One partially preserved written Spanish account, its pages water‑damaged and missing entire sections, describing "streaks of light in the sky" and "a ring of fire around a black eye".
Additional rumours exist, though none are considered reliable. Some mention distant rainstorms, others describe brown, towering pillars of rock being pulled up through the sand and one particularly dubious tale claims that the sand itself briefly glowed red. Whether any of these refer to the actual phenomenon or to unrelated anomalies is impossible to determine. }}
|- | style="padding:0; vertical-align:top;" colspan="2" |
Other Levels
While undoubtedly there are for more instances of '''Phenomenon 400''' than just the 6 above, no documentation of such happenings in a specific level has been found. Only one source pre-1889 exists: a stone carving depicting many stick-limbed people surrounded by several random carvings, all culminating at a crossed-out circle near the top of the carving. It is believed that this is a rudimentary depiction of ''Bleeding Stars'': the incoherent shapes scattered around being debris, and the circle representing either a black hole and its accretion disk, or a star and it's trail. Either way, this shows that this phenomenon is not a [[Phenomenon 12|recent change]], with the source (found inside of a [[Level 865|Forgotten Era]]) being carbon dated to about 600,000 BC, during the reign of [[Entity 70|Homo Callidus]].
|}
Worryingly, it appears the phenomena has been gaining frequency as- in only 30 years- there has been 3 cases of the phenomena, an outlier amongst the other known and even presumed dates of other phenomena in terms of average occurrences per decade. However, this is likely random and not tied to [[The Decay|any other factor]].
